description: Baseline run completed but metric parser failed on ANSI-colored vitest summaries

asi.hypothesis: Establish full 1.0 sweep baseline after fixing SurrealDB and harness setup.
asi.error: Suites actually ran, but autoresearch.sh parsed all failures as 999 because Vitest summary lines contain ANSI escape codes between numbers and words.
asi.observed_true_counts: query-presets 0 failed/11 passed; trash 0 failed/97 passed; uploads 4 failed/98 passed; joins 28 failed/46 passed/1 skipped; queues 71 failed/1 passed/2 skipped.
asi.rollback_reason: Invalid metric extraction, not an adapter regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Strip ANSI escape codes before summary parsing, then rerun baseline. Expected valid primary baseline around 103 failures if same results reproduce.

description: Valid full 1.0 objective sweep baseline

asi.hypothesis: Use a broad objective 1.0 blocker metric across green guardrails plus remaining uploads/joins/queues suites.
asi.baseline: blocker_failures=103 = uploads 4 + joins 28 + queues 71; query-presets and trash are green.
asi.next_action_hint: Largest objective win is queues: failures stem from seed duplicate email before most queue tests run. Fixing payload_jobs/deleteMany/drop/reset behavior may unlock ~70 tests quickly. Joins next largest; uploads has 2 env paste-url failures plus 2 stale file collision failures.

description: Populate drizzle schema for resetDB

asi.hypothesis: Giving the adapter a drizzle._.schema would let Payload resetDB delete all tables before queue seeding, eliminating duplicate user failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Worse primary metric: queues went from 71 to 72 failures. The helper then treated the adapter as full Drizzle and createDrizzleSnapshot crashed on missing db.drizzle.query[tableName].fullSchema.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not expose a populated drizzle._.schema unless also implementing enough drizzle.query/select snapshot surface. Better options: implement clearDatabase and avoid 'drizzle' branch, or enhance the existing drizzle shim to support snapshot APIs.

description: Implement fuller drizzle snapshot shim for queue reset

asi.hypothesis: A minimal drizzle query/select/insert shim plus schema would let Payload clearAndSeed reset and snapshot queue fixtures correctly.
asi.rollback_reason: Still worse than baseline. resetDB attempted DELETE FROM posts_rels from the exposed schema, but that table was registered in adapter metadata without a matching DEFINE TABLE, causing reset errors and duplicate seed failures.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying this path, either define every table exposed in drizzle._.schema (including *_rels/locales) or only expose actually-defined tables. Avoid partial schema entries that resetDB will delete.

description: Add reset/snapshot-compatible drizzle shim for queue fixtures

asi.hypothesis: Expose a minimal but complete drizzle-compatible schema/query/select/insert shim and define all metadata tables so Payload clearAndSeed can reset and snapshot queue fixtures.
asi.result: queues improved from 71 failed / 1 passed to 35 failed / 37 passed; full 1.0 blocker metric improved from 103 to 67.
asi.learned: Queue failures were substantially caused by reset/snapshot incompatibility, not only updateJobs runtime behavior. Defining all metadata tables exposed in the shim avoids DELETE missing-table errors.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue queues: inspect remaining 35 failures. Likely now true job lifecycle/updateJobs/concurrency semantics rather than seed reset.

description: Normalize drizzle snapshot records before restore

asi.hypothesis: Queue login failures after snapshot restore were caused by raw Surreal record IDs in drizzle snapshot rows, so restored users could not be found by Payload string ID.
asi.result: queues improved from 35 failed / 37 passed to 11 failed / 61 passed; full blocker metric improved from 67 to 43.
asi.learned: Snapshot rows exposed through the drizzle shim must be normalized with normalizeDocument before caching/restoring, otherwise IDs become record objects and auth lookups fail after restore.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect remaining 11 queue failures. They likely concern true queue execution semantics: updateJobs limit/sort, deleteJobOnComplete, retries, cancellation, concurrency/supersedes, or nested task status updates.

description: Try cleaning generated upload files before uploads suite

asi.hypothesis: Cleaning stale generated upload files before the uploads suite would remove filename collision failures and reduce blocker_failures by 2.
asi.rollback_reason: The cleanup pattern was too broad and deleted fixture file animated.webp, causing uploads onInit to fail and uploads_failures=999.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not broad-delete test/uploads files. If addressing uploads noise, only delete known generated static directories or use Payload's seedDB uploadsDir cleanup; first restore fixtures with git checkout in the Payload repo.

description: Treat adapter-populated join fields as non-localized for afterRead

asi.hypothesis: Payload afterRead was stripping or hoisting adapter-populated join pagination objects when the sanitized join field inherited localized=true from its target relationship; marking join fields non-localized should preserve populated join result shapes.
asi.result: joins improved from 28 failed / 46 passed to 25 failed / 49 passed; uploads also improved by one noisy file-collision failure; full blocker metric improved from 43 to 39.
asi.learned: For this adapter, join result objects are already locale-resolved and should not be treated as localized field storage by Payload's afterRead recursion.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins: remaining failures still include collection-array sorting/query shape, access/filter/pagination, and top-level where-by-join timeouts. Consider implementing join-aware where prefilter instead of allowing recursive population loops.

description: Add join page metadata and slicing

asi.hypothesis: Implementing page/start slicing and page metadata for join fields would reduce REST/GraphQL join pagination failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 39 to 40. Joins improved by 1 (25→24 failures), but queues regressed by 1 and uploads noisy failures increased by 1, so under full 1.0 rubric this is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: The pagination change is promising for joins and may be worth reapplying later with a queues guard or after stabilizing noisy uploads/queues. It likely fixed one join pagination case but must not be kept alone under current primary metric.

description: Support nested object join options lookup

asi.hypothesis: Some nested join failures may come from Payload passing joins as nested objects instead of dot-path keys, so resolving join options by walking path segments would restore sort/limit/page for group joins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 39 to 41. Joins did not improve versus best and queues/uploads were noisier in this full sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Nested join options lookup alone is not sufficient; group join sort failure likely comes from Payload afterRead repopulation or adapter result shape rather than missing joinOptions. Investigate targeted join logs before retrying.

description: Clean ignored upload artifacts before uploads conformance sweep

asi.hypothesis: The remaining non-environment upload failures in the full sweep were caused by ignored/generated upload artifacts from prior runs, so cleaning ignored files in test/uploads before the uploads suite should produce clean-env upload results without deleting tracked fixtures.
asi.result: uploads improved from 3 failed / 99 passed at best to 2 failed / 100 passed; blocker_failures improved from 39 to 38. The two remaining upload failures are the known localhost/nginx paste-url status expectations.
asi.learned: Use git clean -fdX scoped to test/uploads, not broad file deletion. This removes ignored generated artifacts while preserving tracked fixture files such as animated.webp.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with true adapter blockers: joins 25 failures and queues 11 failures. Uploads can be considered clean-env green except for the documented local nginx paste-url issue.

description: Use Surreal atomic append for top-level $push updates

asi.hypothesis: Queue parallel task log loss was caused by read-merge-write races for top-level $push updates, so translating $push into Surreal atomic array append should preserve concurrent log entries.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 38 to 60; queues regressed from 11 to 33 failures. Atomic append via SET log += [item] caused updateJob calls to return null / jobs to lose expected log state in queue execution paths.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use naive Surreal += for $push. If revisiting concurrent log updates, test Surreal array semantics in isolation and ensure UPDATE returns the updated full document. A safer path may be transactional compare/merge or dedicated log sub-records, but avoid changing this without targeted queue tests first.

description: Use lexicographic join sorting to match Payload string sort semantics

asi.hypothesis: Several join sort failures were caused by adapter-side join sorting using numeric string collation, which orders titles like 'test 14' after 'test 9' differently than Payload/DB lexicographic string sort expectations.
asi.result: joins improved from 25 failed / 49 passed to 21 failed / 53 passed; full blocker metric improved from 38 to 35 despite one noisy queue failure regression.
asi.learned: Join field sorting should use plain lexicographic string comparison, not numeric collation. Numeric-aware sorting overfits human-natural ordering and breaks Payload's expected string order for titles with embedded numbers.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures now likely include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filters, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Honor join page offsets and pagination metadata

asi.hypothesis: After lexicographic sorting stabilized join order, implementing adapter-side join page offsets and metadata should fix REST join pagination without the earlier noisy regressions.
asi.result: joins improved from 21 failed / 53 passed to 20 failed / 54 passed; full blocker metric improved from 35 to 33.
asi.learned: Join pagination needs start=(page-1)*limit slicing plus hasPrevPage/hasNextPage/page/pagingCounter metadata. This is now a real improvement when combined with correct string sort semantics.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Apply join where filters during adapter join fetches

asi.hypothesis: Join field `where` config and requested join `where` options were not applied in adapter-side join population, causing filter-specific join tests to return all joined docs.
asi.result: joins improved from 20 failed / 54 passed to 18 failed / 56 passed; full blocker metric improved from 33 to 32 despite one noisy queue regression.
asi.learned: Adapter join fetches need to compile join where filters into the target collection SELECT before grouping by parent. This directly fixes join filtering cases and is not benchmark-specific.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining likely include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts. Consider merging field.where with joinOptions.where instead of choosing one if a remaining failure shows combined filters are wrong.

description: Handle relationTo filters in polymorphic join queries

asi.hypothesis: Polymorphic join where clauses include virtual relationTo predicates that should select which target collections are queried, not be compiled as fields on target documents.
asi.result: joins improved from 18 failed / 56 passed to 15 failed / 59 passed; full blocker metric improved from 32 to 29 despite one noisy queue regression.
asi.learned: For adapter-populated polymorphic joins, relationTo is result metadata. The adapter must consume relationTo filters per target collection and remove matching predicates before compiling the target collection WHERE clause.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter shape, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Resolve localized join values by request locale

asi.hypothesis: Localized join failures may be caused by flattenJoinValues scanning all locales or defaulting incorrectly instead of using the request locale when resolving localized relationship arrays.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at 29, so no objective improvement. It did not reduce localized join failures in the full 1.0 sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized join failures are likely not simply locale selection in flattenJoinValues. Investigate target document locale flattening / fallback and version-draft join sources before retrying.

description: Reduce client-side virtual join population depth

asi.hypothesis: Top-level where-by-join tests time out because client-side virtual filtering populates relationship/join graphs to depth 5; depth 1 may be sufficient for join field predicates and reduce recursive work.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at 29 despite faster runtime, so per full 1.0 rubric this is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Depth reduction improved duration but not failures. To fix top-level join where tests, implement true join-aware parent prefiltering or improve docMatchesWhere for join pagination objects rather than only reducing population depth.

description: Traverse join pagination docs in getValueAtPath

asi.hypothesis: Top-level where-by-join failures might come from docMatchesWhere/getValueAtPath not traversing join pagination objects like {docs:[...]} for paths such as relatedPosts.title or relatedPosts equals postID.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 29. The added traversal did not reduce join failures, so top-level where-by-join timeouts are likely elsewhere (possibly whereUsesVirtual forcing too much work or join predicates not being converted to target-parent filters).
asi.next_action_hint: Implement a true join-aware parent prefilter: detect where roots that are join fields, query target collections using the suffix predicate plus join.on relationship back to parent, then filter parent docs by matching IDs. Avoid relying solely on populated docs traversal.

description: Make join-aware client filtering traverse pagination docs at shallow depth

asi.hypothesis: Top-level where-by-join failures require both shallow join population to avoid recursive work and getValueAtPath traversal through join pagination objects; each alone was insufficient, but together should let docMatchesWhere evaluate populated joins quickly.
asi.result: joins improved from 15 failed / 59 passed to 8 failed / 66 passed; full blocker metric improved from 29 to 22 and runtime dropped from ~1298s to ~598s.
asi.learned: Client-side virtual handling for join predicates needs only depth 1 and must treat {docs:[...]} as the traversable value. Deep population caused timeouts; plain traversal without shallow depth did not fix them.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with remaining 8 joins and 12 queues. Inspect remaining joins list; likely localized/versioned joins and collection-array sort/filter remain after top-level where-by-join improvements.

description: Preserve requested join sort separately from default sort

asi.hypothesis: Collection-array join sort failure might be caused by losing whether sort was explicitly requested versus defaulted, so preserving requestedSort could avoid collection fallback ordering overriding explicit sort.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 22; no objective failure reduction.
asi.next_action_hint: Collection-array sort failure remains. Need inspect actual sort path and field.collection array result sorting, not just requested/default sort distinction.

description: Traverse localized wrappers in join target path resolution

asi.hypothesis: Localized array join failures were caused by relationship getValueAtPath not traversing localized wrapper objects like { en: [{ category }] } when resolving join.on paths such as localizedArray.category.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 22. Joins improved by one, but queues improved/noise and joins regressed relative to best 8; no full-rubric improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: The localized wrapper traversal is directionally useful but not sufficient under the current aggregate metric. Reconsider later with another complementary joins fix, or run targeted joins-only validation before reapplying.

description: Populate joins after materializing draft query results

asi.hypothesis: Draft query results are materialized from version.version after the version table read, so join fields on the base collection can be missing unless transformRelationshipReads is applied again to the materialized draft docs.
asi.result: Full blocker metric improved from 22 to 19. Joins improved from 8 failures / 66 passed to 5 failures / 69 passed; green guardrails stayed green and queues were unchanged.
asi.learned: The version-table transform path was not sufficient for draft query joins. Explicitly populating relationships/joins on the materialized base draft documents fixes draft join cases without special-casing tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining joins are likely localized relationship joins, GraphQL group pagination, and multi-collection sort/filter. Inspect latest joins log before selecting next fix.

description: Support nested object join options lookup after draft join fixes

asi.hypothesis: Remaining GraphQL group join pagination might be caused by sanitized joins sometimes being passed as nested objects instead of dot-path keys, so resolving join options by walking path segments should restore nested limit/page/sort.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 19; no objective reduction in remaining blocker failures.
asi.next_action_hint: GraphQL group pagination failure is not fixed by nested join option lookup. Inspect actual joins shape or afterRead/GraphQL layer interaction before retrying. Remaining objective wins may be easier in queues retry/cancel/updateJobs semantics.

description: Pass request locale into join value resolution

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized join failures may be caused by resolving localized relationship wrapper objects without the request locale, so passing args.locale into join population and flattenJoinValues should filter relationship IDs to the active locale.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 19 and localized join failure count did not improve in the full objective sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized failures are not solved by simply passing locale into flattenJoinValues. Investigate Payload afterRead locale behavior for target docs and localized relationship sort/filter semantics, or switch focus to queue retry/cancel semantics for larger remaining objective wins.

description: Traverse localized wrappers in join path resolution after draft fixes

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized array join failures may be caused by relationship join path resolution not traversing localized wrapper objects like { en: [{ category }] } for join.on paths.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 19. The run traded one fewer queue failure/noise for one additional joins failure versus best, so no objective improvement under the full 1.0 rubric.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized wrapper traversal remains insufficient and can regress joins. Shift focus to queue retry/cancel/updateJobs semantics or inspect exact remaining join data shape before another localized join attempt.

description: Serialize same-job updates to avoid queue state races

asi.hypothesis: Several queue failures, especially parallel-task and cancellation/retry state transitions, may be caused by concurrent read-merge-write updates to the same payload-jobs document losing fields. Serializing same-job updates in-process should preserve Mongo-like document update semantics for the local queue runner without changing Payload tests.
asi.result: Full blocker metric improved from 19 to 18. Queues improved from 12 failures / 60 passed to 11 failures / 61 passed while joins and green guardrails stayed unchanged.
asi.learned: At least one remaining queue blocker is sensitive to same-job update races; a narrow per-job update lock is safer than the previous Surreal atomic append attempt and preserved existing suites.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect current queue failure list to see which race-sensitive test turned green. Continue with queue semantics: retry count/backoff/cancel/supersedes remain, and a more complete transactional compare/update or targeted updateJobs claim behavior may reduce more failures.

description: Serialize conditional payload job deletions with job update locks

asi.hypothesis: Supersedes queue failures may be caused by payload_jobs deleteMany racing with runJobs marking a job as processing; serializing conditional job deletions with the same per-job lock and rechecking the where clause should avoid deleting newly-running jobs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 18 to 19; queues regressed from 11 to 12 failures.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep broad locked deleteMany path. The supersedes/running race hypothesis was not validated in the full sweep. Continue with more targeted queue retry/backoff/updateJobs semantics or inspect exact current queue failure diff after the kept per-job update lock.

description: Default localized join flattening to default locale

asi.hypothesis: Localized join failures may occur because default-locale requests pass no explicit locale into adapter join population, causing flattenJoinValues to scan all locale wrappers and include es-only relationships in en results. Defaulting join flattening to Payload's defaultLocale should filter localized relationship IDs unless a request locale overrides it.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 18; no objective failure reduction over the current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Default-locale flattening alone is not enough. Remaining localized failures likely involve target doc locale read transforms or localized join.on path resolution. Consider queue retry/backoff semantics next because the joins attempts are plateauing.

name: Payload SurrealDB full 1.0 readiness and modularity

description: Baseline for expanded full 1.0 readiness objective with modularity metrics

asi.hypothesis: Broaden autoresearch to the full ROADMAP-1.0 suite set plus plugin readiness and code modularity tie-breaker metrics, then establish a fresh baseline before continuing optimization.
asi.baseline: Expanded blocker_failures=1045. Most of the new total comes from plugin_nested_docs_failures=999, which indicates suite execution or summary parsing/setup failure rather than necessarily 999 assertions; plugin_search has 17 failures, database 4, collections_graphql 1, relationships 6, joins 5, uploads 2, queues 11.
asi.result: Expanded objective and modularity extraction were committed as the new baseline. Green core suites mostly remained green, but the broader objective reveals additional 1.0 blockers outside the previous active sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: First inspect /tmp/payload-surrealdb-plugin_nested_docs-autoresearch.log.clean to determine why plugin_nested_docs parsed as 999. Fix harness/setup if it is an environment/parser issue; otherwise address true plugin nested docs blockers. Then tackle plugin_search failures and newly exposed database/relationships regressions.

description: Count setup-time failed suites instead of crash sentinel

asi.hypothesis: The expanded baseline was inflated because plugin-nested-docs times out in beforeAll and reports a failed suite, not 999 individual test failures. Counting Vitest's Failed Suites total when no per-test failure count is available makes blocker_failures reflect real objective failures while still penalizing setup blockers.
asi.result: blocker_failures dropped from 1045 to 48 by measuring plugin_nested_docs_failures as 1 failed suite instead of the crash sentinel 999. This is a metric correctness fix, not a product behavior fix. Other suite counts are broadly unchanged, with queues showing one noisy extra failure in this run.
asi.learned: plugin-nested-docs is a true setup-time blocker: beforeAll times out during seeding and all 11 tests are skipped. It should be investigated next as a functional/plugin compatibility issue, not as a parser crash.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect plugin-nested-docs seeding and nestedDocs hooks for recursive breadcrumb/update behavior causing init timeout. Next largest true blocker is plugin_search with 17 failures, followed by queues, relationships, joins, database, uploads, and collections_graphql.

description: Include transaction snapshots in findOne reads

asi.hypothesis: Plugin-search failures come from afterChange hooks calling payload.findByID for a document created in the same transaction. The adapter's find path already merges transaction snapshots, but findOne/findByID did not, causing NotFound before commit.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from 48 to 37. plugin_search improved from 17 failures / 3 passed to 6 failures / 14 passed. Core green suites stayed green; queues remained noisy at 12 failures.
asi.learned: Read-your-writes support was incomplete: findOne must merge queued transaction docs and re-evaluate the where clause against the merged set, not just direct Surreal SELECT results.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue plugin_search: inspect remaining 6 failures. They likely involve reindex/delete/localized edge cases after the basic afterChange findByID path was fixed. Also investigate plugin-nested-docs beforeAll timeout separately.

description: Record transaction snapshots for queued updates

asi.hypothesis: Queued transaction updates returned updated docs to the caller but did not update the transaction read snapshot, so later same-transaction reads such as plugin-search beforeSync could see stale content after an update.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from 37 to 35. plugin_search improved from 6 failures / 14 passed to 5 failures / 15 passed, and queues returned to 11 failures / 61 passed.
asi.learned: Read-your-writes requires both create and update paths to maintain transaction snapshots. Adding snapshots for queued updateOne paths fixes at least one plugin-search update synchronization failure without regressing core green suites.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue plugin_search remaining failures: reindex behavior still returns missing search docs for whole collection/localized/draft cases, and one published-with-new-draft delete expectation remains. Inspect plugin-search reindex endpoint and adapter find/update/delete behavior under localized/versioned docs.

description: Use array intersect for hasMany contains equality

asi.hypothesis: The database hasMany select contains failure may be caused by Surreal array CONTAINS doing partial string matching, so using array::intersect for hasMany equals/contains should enforce exact membership.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 35 to 37. The database failure count did not improve, while queues regressed noisily from 11 to 13 failures.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep the broad hasMany contains rewrite. The failing select-hasMany contains case may have a different cause, possibly field config lookup or Surreal array semantics for select fields only. Prefer investigating with a targeted database-suite reproduction before retrying.

description: Use transaction snapshot as existing doc for direct ID updates

asi.hypothesis: Plugin-search reindex creates a search doc and then updates it for additional locales within the same transaction. Direct update-by-id should use the transaction snapshot as the existing doc instead of falling back to an empty {id} shell when the doc has not committed yet.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 35 and no suite-level failure reduction. The added code also slightly worsened modularity metrics, so it is not worth keeping as a refactor tie-breaker.
asi.next_action_hint: The remaining plugin-search reindex failures are not fixed by direct-id update snapshots alone. Investigate whether transaction commit ordering, localized search doc updates, or reindex delete/create logic is losing search docs after commit.

description: Use INSIDE for scalar hasMany contains filters

asi.hypothesis: Surreal's array CONTAINS semantics may not be exact for hasMany fields in generated WHERE clauses; using scalar INSIDE (array ?? []) for scalar contains/equals should avoid partial string matching while preserving array CONTAINSANY for list values.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 35 to 36 and database/relationships failures were unchanged. Queues regressed noisily by one failure, so this is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop trying broad hasMany operator rewrites without targeted evidence. The database contains failure likely comes from field config/path handling rather than Surreal scalar membership syntax, or the suite result is masked by another database failure set.

description: Extract pagination helpers from operations

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of pagination calculation/result shaping from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve modularity while preserving the same conformance state.
asi.rollback_reason: Although modularity_penalty improved from 2070 to 2042 and operations_lines dropped from 1335 to 1321, primary blocker_failures worsened from best 35 to 36 due to a queue failure count increase. Per objective rules, conformance regression wins over modularity, so discard.
asi.next_action_hint: The pagination extraction is likely behavior-preserving and may be worth retrying only if queue noise is confirmed, but do not keep it under a worse primary metric. Continue with functional fixes, preferably plugin-search or plugin-nested-docs.

description: Retry pagination helper extraction for modularity confirmation

asi.hypothesis: The previous pagination helper extraction likely preserved behavior and only lost to queue noise; rerunning the same modularity extraction should confirm whether it can meet the equal-conformance tie-breaker.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric again worsened from best 35 to 36 with queues at 12 failures, so it cannot be kept despite improved modularity. The repeated result suggests either queue noise is consistently unfavorable in these runs or the change subtly affects timing enough to expose an extra queue failure.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry pagination extraction again under the current objective. Continue with true functional fixes in plugin-search, plugin-nested-docs, relationships, or database virtual/contains behavior.

description: Use exact array membership for client-side contains

asi.hypothesis: The hasMany select and relationship contains failures are evaluated through client-side filtering because whereUsesVirtual routes hasMany fields through docMatchesWhere. Client-side contains should use exact array membership instead of substring matching for array values.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 35 to 36. Database improved by 1 and relationships improved by 1, but fields regressed from 0 to 3 failures, so the broad array exactness change breaks field contains semantics elsewhere.
asi.next_action_hint: Need schema-aware client-side contains: exact membership for hasMany select/relationship arrays, but preserve substring contains for text/richText-like array paths used by fields suite. Add field-aware operator handling rather than blanket Array exact matching.

description: Use schema-aware exact contains for hasMany client filters

asi.hypothesis: Client-side contains should be exact only for schema-known hasMany select/relationship/upload fields, preserving substring matching for field arrays that represent text-like paths.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 35, so no objective improvement. Database improved by 1 and relationships improved by 1 with fields preserved, but queue noise/regression from 11 to 13 offset the gains. Modularity also worsened, so no tie-breaker applies.
asi.next_action_hint: This change is semantically promising and may be worth combining with a queue stabilizer or rerunning once to distinguish noise, but do not keep it alone. It fixes the targeted database/relationship contains behavior without field regressions.

description: Use schema-aware exact contains for hasMany client filters

asi.hypothesis: Client-side contains should be exact only for schema-known hasMany select/relationship/upload fields, preserving substring matching for text-like fields. Prior run showed targeted database/relationship gains but was obscured by queue noise; rerun to confirm.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 35 to 34. Database improved from 4 to 3 failures and relationships from 6 to 5, fields stayed green, while queues were one worse than best but less noisy than prior attempt.
asi.learned: Schema-aware client-side contains is a valid semantic fix. Broad exact array contains regressed fields, but restricting exactness to hasMany select/relationship/upload fields preserves field contains semantics and fixes two blocker tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue database/relationships: remaining failures include nested virtual/relationship querying and relationship sorting. Consider expanding schema-aware client filtering for deeper relationship paths or fixing virtual alias population.

description: Use depth 2 for client-side virtual filtering

asi.hypothesis: Remaining database virtual-field and relationship nested-query failures need populated relationship chains two levels deep during client-side virtual filtering/sorting. Depth 1 was enough for join where predicates but insufficient for virtual aliases such as post.category.title.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 34 to 33. Database became green (3→0 failures) and relationships improved substantially (5→1), while joins regressed from 5 to 12 and runtime increased. Net primary still improved by 1.
asi.learned: Client-side virtual handling needs depth 2 for general relationship virtuals. However applying depth 2 globally to all client virtual paths over-populates join where cases and regresses joins, so a future refinement should compute depth by virtual alias/path type: depth 2 for relationship virtual paths, depth 1 for join-field predicates.
asi.next_action_hint: Refine client virtual population depth dynamically to recover join regressions while preserving database/relationship gains. Detect join-root where predicates and keep depth 1 for those; use depth 2 only for virtual aliases / relationship nested paths.

description: Use shallow client depth for join-field predicates

asi.hypothesis: Depth 2 is needed for ordinary relationship virtual paths, but join-field predicates should keep shallow depth 1 to avoid recursive over-population and the join regressions introduced by the global depth-2 change.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 33 to 28. Database stayed green, relationships stayed at 1 failure, and joins recovered from 12 failures back to 5. Queues were noisy at 13 failures, but the net primary improvement was large.
asi.learned: Client-side virtual population depth must be path-sensitive. Join predicates need shallow population through join pagination docs; ordinary virtual relationship paths need depth 2 for nested relationship values.
asi.next_action_hint: This added pathContainsJoinField/whereUsesJoinField logic inside operations.ts and increased modularity_penalty. Consider later extracting path/field introspection helpers once conformance is stable. Continue with plugin_search remaining 5 failures or plugin_nested_docs setup timeout.

description: Use depth 3 for non-join client virtual filtering

asi.hypothesis: After separating join predicates back to shallow depth, the remaining relationships failure is a 4x-deep relationship where path (`movie.director.movie.name`) that needs depth 3 for non-join client-side virtual filtering.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 28 to 27. Relationships suite is now green (1→0 failures / 57 passed). Database remains green; joins remain at 5 failures. Queues are noisy at 13 failures but the primary still improved.
asi.learned: Non-join client-side relationship/virtual filtering needs depth 3 to cover Payload's 4x-deep relationship query semantics. Join predicates still need depth 1, so the path-sensitive split remains important.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining blockers: plugin_search reindex/delete/localization (5), joins localized/GraphQL/collection-array (5), uploads env (2), collections_graphql multi-sort (1), plugin_nested_docs setup timeout (1), queues noisy/lifecycle (currently 13). Consider fixing GraphQL multi-sort or plugin_search next.

description: Preserve raw comma sort segments and fallback sort

asi.hypothesis: Payload's sort sanitizer splits comma strings without trimming, and Mongo preserves leading whitespace. GraphQL multi-sort `sort: "title, number"` therefore treats the second segment as an invalid raw field and falls back to createdAt desc. The Surreal adapter was trimming segments and incorrectly sorting by number asc.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 27 to 25. collections_graphql is now green (1→0 failures / 47 passed), and the dedicated sort suite stayed green. Queues were noisy but still net improved.
asi.learned: Sort parsing must match Payload/Mongo raw comma segment behavior. Filtering empty segments is fine, but trimming changes GraphQL semantics for comma-space sort strings. Existing fallback `createdAt DESC` then provides stable order when a raw segment is invalid.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining blockers are plugin_search=5, joins=5, uploads=2, plugin_nested_docs setup=1, and queues noise/lifecycle. Focus plugin_search reindex/delete or plugin_nested_docs timeout next.

description: Track transaction deletes in read snapshots

asi.hypothesis: Plugin-search reindex deletes existing search docs and then recreates or updates search docs in the same Payload transaction. The adapter recorded transaction create/update snapshots but not delete tombstones, so same-transaction reads could still see DB rows already scheduled for deletion and update those soon-to-be-deleted rows instead of the newly created search index.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 25 to 20. plugin_search improved from 5 failures to 1 failure, with reindex cases now passing. Queues also ran at 11 failures instead of 12. All green guardrails stayed green.
asi.learned: Read-your-writes transaction semantics need delete visibility, not just create/update snapshots. Queued transactional deletes must be represented as tombstones and applied to find/findOne/count before merging in transaction-created docs.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect remaining plugin_search failure; likely the published-to-draft deleteDrafts/version query path. Other remaining blockers: joins=5 localized/group/collection-array, uploads=2 local paste-url env, plugin_nested_docs setup=1, queues=11 lifecycle/noise. The new transaction delete tracking increased operations.ts size; refactor transaction visibility helpers later after conformance stabilizes.

description: Use transaction snapshot as existing doc for direct ID updates after delete tombstones

asi.hypothesis: Now that transaction delete tombstones fixed plugin-search reindex, direct update-by-id might still need to merge against the transaction snapshot rather than the stale committed DB row during same-request version/search updates.
asi.result: No plugin_search improvement: it remained at 1 failure, and queue noise raised blocker_failures from best 20 to 21.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened and the targeted plugin-search failure did not change. The change also increased operations.ts size/modularity penalty.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry direct-ID transaction snapshot merging as a standalone fix. Investigate the remaining plugin_search published-to-draft deleteDrafts failure by determining why the plugin's draft:false published lookup still finds a published doc after unpublishing, or shift to joins/plugin_nested_docs/queues.

description: Resolve localized join path wrappers by locale

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized join failures may come from resolving join.on paths through localized wrapper objects such as {en: [...]}; join value collection should select the request/default locale instead of failing path traversal or flattening all locales.
asi.result: Joins improved from 5 to 4 failures, but queues regressed noisily from best 11 to 13 and primary blocker_failures worsened from best 20 to 21.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary conformance metric worsened despite one join improvement, and the change increased relationships.ts size/modularity penalty. Under the full 1.0 rubric it cannot be kept alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized join wrapper handling is promising but needs to be combined with a net primary improvement or queue stabilization. Consider targeting queues/plugin_nested_docs/plugin_search remaining failure next, or reapply localized join handling only with an additional fix that offsets queue noise.

description: Use default locale when resolving localized join wrappers

asi.hypothesis: Localized join.on paths can traverse localized wrapper objects like {en: [...]} before the relationship field segment. Resolving those wrappers with Payload's default locale should allow default-locale join population without flattening unrelated locales.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 20 to 19. joins improved from 5 to 4 failures while queues stayed at the best observed 11 failures. All green guardrails stayed green.
asi.learned: A minimal default-locale localized wrapper resolution is enough to fix one localized join case without passing request locale through the full relationship read API. The broader request-locale variant also improved joins but was noisier and more invasive.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining joins failures are likely explicit non-default locale localized joins, GraphQL group join pagination timeout, and collection-array ordering. Consider extending locale-aware join resolution only where request locale is safely available, or focus plugin_search remaining deleteDrafts failure / plugin_nested_docs setup timeout / queues lifecycle.

description: Re-filter transaction-merged find results by where

asi.hypothesis: When a transactional update changes a document so it no longer matches the original SQL WHERE, find() can query the old committed row, merge in the transaction snapshot, and return the snapshot without rechecking the predicate. Re-filtering transaction-merged results should make same-transaction draft/published lookups obey read-your-writes semantics.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 19 to 18. plugin_search is now green (1→0 failures), and plugin_nested_docs also completed green in this run instead of timing out. Joins stayed at 4 failures; queues were noisy at 12 failures but net primary still improved.
asi.learned: Transaction read snapshots need post-merge predicate filtering for ordinary non-virtual find(), just like count/findOne already did. This fixes published-to-draft search deletion semantics and may reduce hook recursion/timeouts by exposing consistent same-transaction reads.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining objective failures are joins=4, uploads=2 local paste-url env, and queues≈12 lifecycle/noise. Focus joins GraphQL group pagination timeout / collection-array ordering, or queue retry/cancel/supersedes semantics. Consider refactoring transaction visibility helpers later because operations.ts is now very large.

description: Serialize updateJobs selection and mutation

asi.hypothesis: Queue worker concurrency can race inside updateJobs because it performs a find selection followed by per-job updates. Serializing updateJobs selection and mutation with the existing job update lock should reduce duplicate/stale job claiming without changing Payload's job semantics.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 18 to 17. Queue failures improved from the prior run's 12 to 11 while plugin-search and plugin-nested-docs stayed green. All guardrails remained green.
asi.learned: Some remaining queue failures are sensitive to the non-atomic select-then-update window in updateJobs. A coarse in-process updateJobs lock is conservative but aligns this adapter's local runner semantics with atomic job claiming expectations better than leaving selection races exposed.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue queues: remaining 11 failures still include retry/backoff/cancellation/parallel-log/supersedes cases. Investigate lost concurrent log updates or updateMany/updateJobs atomicity more specifically. Joins=4 and uploads=2 also remain.

description: Use sort direction for collection-array join tie ordering

asi.hypothesis: Collection-array joins need deterministic cross-collection fallback ordering when the requested sort path compares equal or is unavailable on wrapper docs. The fallback should respect the requested sort direction instead of always sorting relationTo descending.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 17 to 16. joins improved from 4 to 3 failures while queues remained at 11 failures and plugin suites stayed green.
asi.learned: The remaining collection-array join ordering failure was caused by cross-collection tie handling, not by missing join options. Direction-aware relationTo fallback matches both ascending and descending collection-array join sort expectations.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining joins are localized request-locale cases and GraphQL group pagination timeout. Queues still have 11 retry/backoff/cancel/parallel/supersedes failures, and uploads has 2 local paste-url failures.

description: Pass request locale into localized join wrapper resolution

asi.hypothesis: After default-locale wrapper resolution and collection-array sort fixes, localized join resolution can safely use the request locale when one is available, while falling back to default locale otherwise. This should make explicit non-default locale join population choose es/de relationship wrappers instead of always using en.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from best 16 to 15. Queues were favorable at 10 failures, and all plugin/green guardrail suites stayed green. Joins remained at 3 failures in aggregate, so the observed primary improvement came from queue noise/improvement rather than a counted join reduction.
asi.learned: Passing request locale through relationship read transforms is now safe under the full sweep and does not regress green suites. However current remaining join failures did not decrease, suggesting those explicit localized cases may also need target doc locale transforms or requested locale propagation elsewhere in Payload afterRead/GraphQL paths.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect current joins log to see whether localized failure messages changed. Remaining objective: joins=3, queues=10, uploads=2. Queue retries/backoff/cancel remain the largest failure cluster; GraphQL group pagination timeout is the largest join blocker.

description: Support nested object lookup for join options

asi.hypothesis: GraphQL group join pagination timeout may still be caused by sanitized joins being provided as nested objects (group.relatedPosts) instead of dot-path keys, so resolving join options by walking nested join option objects should restore limit/sort for grouped joins.
asi.result: No objective improvement. joins stayed at 3 failures and queues worsened from best 10 to 11, raising blocker_failures from best 15 to 16.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened and the targeted GraphQL grouped join timeout did not improve. The helper also increased relationships.ts size/modularity penalty.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry nested join option lookup alone. Investigate GraphQL group pagination timeout elsewhere, likely afterRead repopulation/recursive depth or GraphQL resolver behavior. Queue failures remain the largest cluster and may be more productive.

description: Serialize all payload job mutations behind one lock

asi.hypothesis: The remaining queue failures may come from updateJobs, direct job updates, and supersedes deletes racing through separate lock keys. Serializing all payload-jobs update/delete mutations behind a single in-process lock should provide Mongo-like single-document visibility for the local worker and prevent lost task logs or deletion of newly-processing jobs.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures was unchanged at 15. queues stayed at the best observed 10 failures, joins stayed at 3, and uploads stayed at 2, but modularity and file-size metrics worsened and runtime increased.
asi.rollback_reason: No conformance improvement over the current best and the change worsened operations.ts size/modularity. A global job mutation lock is too broad to keep without reducing failures.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry broad global payload-jobs serialization. Target a specific queue semantic instead: retry/backoff nextRunAt and totalTried behavior, cancellation state transitions, or supersedes delete predicates/processing visibility.

description: Use scalar INSIDE for non-array in/not_in filters

asi.hypothesis: Queue concurrency filtering may not exclude running concurrency keys because scalar NOT IN is compiled with Surreal's collection membership syntax incorrectly. Using scalar INSIDE/negated INSIDE for non-array in/not_in filters should make running-key exclusion precise without changing hasMany array filters.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 17. queues regressed from best 10 to 12 failures, while joins and uploads were unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: The scalar INSIDE rewrite did not fix queue concurrency and made the queue suite worse. Existing IN/NOT IN behavior is not the cause of the remaining concurrency-control failures, or the semantic change affected other queue selection predicates adversely.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry broad scalar in/not_in rewrites. Focus retry/backoff and cancellation update semantics or inspect specific updateJobs data shapes for totalTried/taskStatus persistence.

description: Apply payload job updates immediately outside transaction queue

asi.hypothesis: Payload's queue utility starts a transaction around each job update, but adapter per-job locks release when updateOne queues the statement, before Payload commits the transaction. Executing payload-jobs updates immediately instead of queuing them should keep the existing same-job lock around the actual write and reduce lost log/retry updates.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 16. queues were 11 instead of the best 10, with joins and uploads unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Bypassing transaction queuing for payload-jobs updates did not reduce queue failures and slightly worsened the primary metric. It may alter Payload's expected job update transaction boundaries without addressing the true retry/concurrency issues.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry immediate payload-jobs updates as a broad fix. Inspect exact queue update data and where predicates for retry/backoff or cancellation; target a smaller semantic such as treating completedAt:null as absent for runnable jobs or preserving array log pushes atomically.

description: Serialize commits that update payload jobs

asi.hypothesis: Per-job update locks release after queuing a transaction statement, while the actual write happens during commit. Serializing commits that touch payload-jobs should reduce lost concurrent job log/retry updates without bypassing Payload's transaction boundary.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 16. queues were 11 instead of the best 10, with joins and uploads unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Serializing payload-jobs commits did not improve queue failures and added complexity. The remaining queue blockers are not solved by commit-level serialization alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid broader serialization. Target a concrete data-shape issue: atomic top-level log $push for parallel tasks, completedAt/null existence semantics, or supersedes delete rechecking against current processing state.

description: Use Surreal array concat for atomic job log pushes

asi.hypothesis: Parallel queue task failures lose log entries because top-level log $push updates are read-merge-write rather than atomic. Translating only log $push to Surreal array::concat should preserve concurrent append semantics while leaving broader $push behavior unchanged.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened badly from best 15 to 40. queues regressed to 34 failures and versions regressed by 1, while joins/uploads were unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: Surreal array::concat atomic assignment is not compatible with the queue update path as implemented, likely because UPDATE returns malformed/null job docs or the expression syntax does not preserve full job shape. This repeats the earlier lesson that naive Surreal atomic append variants are unsafe.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry Surreal atomic log append without an isolated Surreal syntax probe and a targeted queue-only validation. Focus instead on non-atomic semantics: completedAt/null existence, retry waitUntil selection, or supersedes delete recheck.

description: Extract atomic update path helpers

asi.hypothesis: With queue functional fixes plateauing, a behavior-preserving extraction of atomic update path helpers from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve modularity without changing conformance.
asi.result: Architecture metrics improved substantially (modularity_penalty 2233→2111, operations_lines 1400→1339, largest_src_file_lines 1400→1339), but primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 17 due to queues at 12 failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Per experiment rules, conformance regression outweighs modularity improvement. The extraction appears behavior-preserving but cannot be kept while the full sweep is worse than the current best.
asi.next_action_hint: This extraction is a good refactor candidate to retry only when queue noise is equal to best or after queue failures are stabilized. Continue with targeted queue semantics, especially completedAt/null existence or supersedes processing visibility.

description: Add deterministic id tie-breaker to SQL sorting

asi.hypothesis: The remaining LIFO queue failure may come from equal or near-equal createdAt values being sorted nondeterministically. Adding an id tie-breaker in the same direction as createdAt should make FIFO/LIFO queue selection deterministic without changing primary sort semantics except on ties.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 17. queues were 12, joins and uploads unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: The deterministic id tie-breaker did not improve queue ordering failures and increased operations.ts size/modularity penalty. LIFO/concurrency failures are likely not caused by SQL tie ordering alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry generic SQL tie-breakers. Focus queue retry/cancellation state persistence or targeted supersedes processing visibility. Consider targeted queue-only diagnostics before the next full sweep to avoid noisy broad hypotheses.

description: Use Surreal IS NONE for exists filters

asi.hypothesis: Queue runnable and supersedes predicates rely heavily on exists:false for completedAt/concurrencyKey. Surreal's IS NONE syntax might more accurately match missing or null fields than equality against NONE/NULL.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened badly from best 15 to 25. fields regressed by 3 and trash regressed by 6; queues stayed worse than best at 11 failures.
asi.rollback_reason: IS NONE semantics are not equivalent to Payload exists:false/null expectations across the full suite. This regressed green guardrails and did not improve queues.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not change global exists operator semantics. If completedAt/null remains suspicious, handle payload-jobs specifically in queue selection or inspect Surreal rows directly instead of altering all exists filters.

description: Track in-process job processing state for supersedes deletes

asi.hypothesis: Supersedes failures delete jobs that have been marked processing by the local worker but whose DB update may not be visible to the queueing delete yet. Tracking in-process payload job IDs when processing is set true and excluding them from payload-jobs deleteMany should preserve running jobs without changing non-job deletes.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 16. queues remained worse than best at 11 failures and joins/uploads were unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: In-process processing tracking plus client-side payload-jobs deleteMany did not reduce supersedes failures and worsened modularity/operations size. The running-job deletion issue is not solved by this local marker alone, or other queue noise offsets it.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep local processing registry. Need targeted diagnostics for supersedes to inspect the actual processing value and delete predicate timing, or shift back to retry/backoff/cancellation persistence.

description: Disable localized join fallback to first available locale

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized join failures may be caused by localized relationship wrappers falling back to the first available locale when the requested/default locale is absent. For join membership, default-locale requests should not include es-only relationships, and es requests should not fall back to en relationships.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 16. joins remained at 3 failures and queues were 11, so the targeted localized join failures did not improve in the full sweep.
asi.rollback_reason: Removing first-locale fallback in join wrapper resolution did not reduce joins and left primary metric worse due to queue count. The localized failures likely involve earlier target doc read transforms or Payload locale fallback behavior rather than this wrapper fallback alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop localized join fallback tweaks without targeted instrumentation. Queue blockers remain largest; if continuing joins, inspect actual joined doc IDs/titles for the two localized failures before changing resolution again.

description: Pass locale into nested join target population

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized join failures may come from joined target docs being recursively populated/read-transformed without the request locale, so nested join target population should receive the same locale used for the parent join.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 16. joins remained at 3 failures and queues were 11, so no objective improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Passing locale into nested target population did not change the remaining join failures. The localized failures likely concern membership resolution or Payload locale fallback before target population, not nested read transforms.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid more blind localized join changes. Queue failures remain the larger target; for joins, add targeted diagnostics outside full sweep to inspect raw categoriesLocalized storage and join membership IDs.

description: Extract atomic update path helpers for modularity

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of atomic update path helpers from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve modularity while preserving the current best conformance state.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures matched the current best at 15 while architecture metrics improved substantially: modularity_penalty dropped from 2233 to 2111, operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines dropped from 1400 to 1339, with green guardrails preserved.
asi.learned: The atomic update helper extraction is behavior-preserving when queue noise is favorable/equal to best, and cleanly separates dotted operator path handling from the large operations module.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue targeting true conformance blockers: queues remain around 10 failures, joins 3, uploads 2 local env. Future refactors can extract more coherent operations helpers only when full conformance remains equal.

description: Extract pagination calculation helper for modularity

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of pagination calculation from operations.ts into a small utility should further reduce large-file concentration while preserving the current best conformance state.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures matched the best at 15, with all green guardrails preserved. Architecture metrics improved: modularity_penalty 2111→2095 and operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1339→1331.
asi.learned: The pagination helper extraction is behavior-preserving when full-suite queue noise remains at the best observed level. Small coherent extractions are now viable tie-breaker wins while conformance is stable.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue reducing true blockers where possible: queues≈10, joins=3, uploads=2 local env. Additional modular extractions can be kept only when blocker_failures remains equal to best and guardrails stay green.

description: Extract sorting and comparison helpers for modularity

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of sort parsing, SQL sort construction, and comparison helpers from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve module separation and reduce large-file concentration while preserving the current best conformance state.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures matched the best at 15 with all green guardrails preserved. Architecture improved substantially: modularity_penalty 2095→1961 and operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1331→1264.
asi.learned: Sorting/comparison logic is separable from core CRUD operations without changing adapter semantics. This is a strong modularity tie-breaker win under equal conformance.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue true blocker work: queues around 10 failures, joins 3, uploads 2 local env. Further refactors should remain small and only keep when blocker_failures and guardrails remain equal.

description: Extract geospatial query helpers for modularity

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of geospatial parsing/distance/polygon helpers from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve module separation and reduce large-file concentration while preserving the current best conformance state.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures matched the best at 15 with all green guardrails preserved. Architecture improved: modularity_penalty 1961→1871 and operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1264→1219.
asi.learned: Geospatial client-side query logic is separable from core operations without changing semantics, continuing to reduce operations.ts concentration under equal conformance.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with true blockers: queues≈10, joins=3, uploads=2 local env. Consider a small query predicate extraction next only if full conformance remains equal, or run targeted diagnostics for queue retry/cancel state.

description: Extract transaction visibility merge helper

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of transaction visibility merging from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve module separation while preserving current conformance.
asi.result: Architecture metrics improved (modularity_penalty 1871→1847, operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1219→1207), but primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 17 due to queues at 12 failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Conformance regression outweighs modularity improvement under the experiment rules. The extraction is likely behavior-preserving, but queue noise/regression means it cannot be kept now.
asi.next_action_hint: This extraction can be retried only when queues are equal to best. Prefer targeting true queue conformance issues or keep refactors only when blocker_failures remains 15.

description: Extract transaction visibility merge helper

asi.hypothesis: A behavior-preserving extraction of transaction visibility merging from operations.ts into a focused utility should improve module separation while preserving current conformance; the previous discard likely lost only to queue noise.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures matched the best at 15, queues were at the best observed 10 failures, and all green guardrails stayed green. Architecture improved: modularity_penalty 1871→1847 and operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1219→1207.
asi.learned: Transaction visibility merging is separable from operations.ts without changing semantics. Retrying behavior-preserving refactors can be justified when prior discard was due to objective noise and the modularity gain is clear.

description: Extract unique index validation helper

asi.result: Architecture metrics improved (modularity_penalty 1847→1759 and operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1207→1163), but primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 16 due to queues at 11 failures.

description: Retry unique index validation extraction

asi.hypothesis: The previous unique-index extraction was likely behavior-preserving and lost only to queue noise; retrying should confirm whether it can be kept as an equal-conformance modularity improvement.
asi.result: Primary blocker_failures remained worse than best at 16, with queues at 11 failures. Architecture metrics improved but conformance did not match best.

asi.result: Architecture metrics improved substantially (modularity_penalty 1847→1523, operations_lines/largest_src_file_lines 1207→1045), but primary blocker_failures worsened from best 15 to 17 due to queues at 12 failures.
